prompt: Before we touch code, I want a few serious architecture options for a feature that can execute a saved plan, keep progress state, and coordinate code-review agents at the right checkpoints. It needs to work in this skills repo and build on the existing plan/task workflow without becoming a monolith. Please compare alternatives and tell me which direction you'd bet on.
assumptions: Saved plan means the existing .agents/scratchpad/<feature>/ artifact set centered on plan.md and tasks.md.
Resume state must survive agent/session interruption and be stored in repo-local or workspace-local artifacts.
Existing skills should remain independently runnable rather than being folded into a single orchestrator.
Review checkpoints should happen after Foundational, after each User Story phase, and at final Polish.
The repo should avoid introducing an external service for execution state at the first step.
